items.title: WOM visible manual production-task create or import entry is not exposed

persistenceCoverage.operation: 定位新建生产工单或制造任务可见入口
persistenceCoverage.api: POST /msService/WOM/produceTask/produceTask/makeTaskList-pending (query only from current visible UI); source-mapped but not UI-triggered manual endpoints: POST /makeTaskEdit/save, POST /makeTaskEdit/submit, GET /importMainXls, GET /downloadXls; accepted daily-plan create endpoint recorded separately: POST /produceTaskCreated2

recheckCommands: manual: after product decision, either expose a real create/import entry and run marker persistence, or mark the visible manual-create entry out of scope with documented product evidence
passCriteria: Product owner confirms whether visible manual create/import is in scope, or whether produceTaskCreated2/day-plan generation is the only supported creation path.
If in scope, runtime/menu/button metadata exposes a real create/import entry without bypassing the product flow.
A browser/API marker action writes wom_produce_tasks and required workflow tables, with PostgreSQL verification.
If out of scope, persistence acceptance is reclassified with explicit product evidence and does not hide the public produceTaskCreated no-op.
nextActions: Confirm the intended product entry for manufacturing task creation.
If manual create/import is required, recover the missing menu/button/runtime entry and capture payload.
If daily-plan generation is intended, document the scope decision and keep produceTaskCreated2 as the accepted creation path.
nonSolutions: Do not mark the visible manual create entry PASS from produceTaskCreated2 alone.
Do not expose a fake save button without a product-confirmed payload and PostgreSQL marker proof.

items.title: public produceTaskCreated explicitly disabled pending product decision

persistenceCoverage.operation: 候选日计划生成制造指令单接口显式禁用探针

recheckCommands: manual: after product decision or implementation recovery, call POST /msService/public/WOM/produceTask/produceTask/produceTaskCreated with a unique ADP_E2E marker and query wom_produce_tasks
passCriteria: Product owner confirms whether the public endpoint is still a supported contract.
If supported, WOMProduceTaskServiceImpl.creatProTask is restored or replaced and a marker request writes wom_produce_tasks plus required workflow tables.
If deprecated, the explicit disabled/failure response is documented as the supported behavior and the endpoint is removed from create acceptance scope.
The accepted replacement path produceTaskCreated2 remains separate and cannot be used to mark this public endpoint PASS.
nextActions: Latest explicit-disabled evidence refreshed at 2026-06-22T07:29:05.735Z with marker ADP_E2E_20260622072905_PUBLIC_PRODUCE_NOOP; endpoint returned HTTP 200/code=400 and PostgreSQL marker count 0 -> 0.
Decide supported-vs-deprecated product status for the public endpoint.
If supported, restore the real create implementation in source and rerun marker API/browser/PostgreSQL acceptance.
If deprecated, keep/document the disabled response and remove it from supported integration docs.
nonSolutions: Do not mark this endpoint PASS from an explicit disabled response.
Do not accept HTTP 200 transport status without checking business code and PostgreSQL marker rows.
Do not count produceTaskCreated2 evidence as proof that the public endpoint works.

latestProbe.verificationSql:
SELECT count(*)::text
FROM public.wom_produce_tasks
WHERE coalesce(table_no::text, '') LIKE '%ADP_E2E_20260622072905_PUBLIC_PRODUCE_NOOP%'
   OR coalesce(produce_batch_num::text, '') LIKE '%ADP_E2E_20260622072905_PUBLIC_PRODUCE_NOOP%'
   OR coalesce(day_plan_ids::text, '') LIKE '%ADP_E2E_20260622072905_PUBLIC_PRODUCE_NOOP%';

items.title: WOM makeTaskList QR code generation endpoint is missing
recheckCommands: manual: after the WOM printManage QR endpoint/package is restored, click 生成二维码 from the real makeTaskList toolbar with a marker WOM task and capture generateQrCode/startPrint/backfill-printInfo requests plus PostgreSQL QR/print table evidence where applicable
passCriteria: The real WOM makeTaskList 生成二维码 toolbar action no longer calls missing 404 printManage endpoints.
The recovered WOM package or source module exposes /WOM/printManage/generateQrCode and any required print/backfill endpoints through the normal gateway path.
A real browser click captures request payload, response body and visible success/failure feedback without raw i18n keys.

If the flow persists QR or print metadata, PostgreSQL marker queries prove target table writes; if it only returns a file/print command, persistence is explicitly marked NOT_APPLICABLE with file/print evidence.
Printer and QR type configuration prerequisites are documented and seeded through normal configuration paths rather than direct ad hoc database writes.
nextActions: Recover or implement the WOM printManage QR endpoint/package that matches the existing toolbar target.
Confirm required printer, QR type and package configuration prerequisites.
Rerun make probe-wom-qrcode-route and the real browser toolbar click smoke.
Update wom-toolbar-action-coverage and persistence/file-response acceptance assets before reclassifying the action.
nonSolutions: Do not count the QR dialog shell opening as PASS while generateCode/generateQrCode still returns 404.
Do not seed printer rows alone and call the flow complete without the missing WOM endpoint implementation.
Do not classify this as a PostgreSQL compatibility gap unless a real restored endpoint reaches a missing table, column, type cast or Oracle dialect issue.
latestEvidenceNotes: 2026-06-22 09:27 reran make probe-wom-qrcode-route against http://100.99.133.43:18080: GET printDate/generateCode, POST generateQrCode and POST backfill-printInfo all returned HTTP 404/code 404; remote WOMMs service jar scan still found 0 printManage/generateQrCode/backfill-printInfo/QrCode class or string matches; PostgreSQL table counts remained baseset_printers=0, baseset_qr_code_types=0, baseset_qr_detail_infos=0 and printer_register=8.

items.title: Batch formula visible manual edit entry requires external Batch client
recheckCommands: manual: provision external Batch client, trigger WebSocket/ActiveX edit flow, then save with an ADP_E2E marker and query rm_formulas
passCriteria: The real external-client driven edit entry opens batchFormulaEdit without a fake platform-only button.
A unique marker proves edit/save persistence in rm_formulas and related RM process/activity tables.
Browser, API, backend and PostgreSQL evidence are added to the acceptance reports.
nextActions: Recover or provision the external Batch client/server workflow.
Capture WebSocket/browser/API evidence for the real edit flow.
Move the production case to PASS only after marker persistence is proven.
nonSolutions: Do not add a fake list button that bypasses the vendor Batch client workflow.
Do not mark PASS from batch/sync import evidence alone.

items.title: QCS bad-quantity and material inventory write-back remain unresolved
recheckCommands: manual: after material service deployment, verify checkProdResult/generateProduceOutSing with marker inventory data and query material stock-in tables
passCriteria: material service has a healthy Nacos prod instance.
checkProdResult and generateProduceOutSing no longer return tenant-service 503.
Marker flow proves inventory or stock-in target table writes in PostgreSQL.
If standalone bad quantity is required, a product package or manual identifies its field, route and target table first.
nextActions: Deploy the material warehouse/inventory package or map the correct serviceName.
Re-run business dependency readiness smoke.
Only reopen standalone bad-quantity acceptance if product/package evidence supplies the missing route/table.
nonSolutions: Do not treat accepted QCS qualified/unqualified WOM state backfill as inventory proof.
Do not invent a bad-quantity database column without a recovered product route/table.

items.title: ProcessAnalysis traceability service is missing
recheckCommands: manual: after ProcessAnalysis deployment, open isProdprocessView/processBatchViewOut from the real WOM trace button with a marker batch
passCriteria: ProcessAnalysis or traceability service has a healthy Nacos prod instance.
ProcessAnalysis runtime_view, menu and table counts are non-zero and mapped to the deployed module.
Authenticated traceability endpoints return successful responses instead of 503.
The real WOM trace button opens processBatchViewOut and target query tables are verified in PostgreSQL where applicable.
nextActions: Recover or deploy the ProcessAnalysis module package.
Apply module runtime views, menus, permissions and PostgreSQL tables.
Run marker browser/API/DB acceptance before changing PROD-020 to PASS.
nonSolutions: Do not patch WOM alone and call traceability done; the target module is absent.
Do not use direct URL reachability without service registration and DB evidence.

items.title: Full report-work remains blocked at bad-quantity scope and material boundary
recheckCommands: manual: rerun accepted WOM report-work marker flows, then add inventory/material marker acceptance once material service is available
passCriteria: Accepted report-work subflows remain green: process start/end, activity start/end, output detail, input detail, easy activity and check activity.
The material/inventory service path is available and marker stock-in or inventory write-back is proven.
If product requires standalone bad quantity, the dedicated field/route/table is recovered and marker-tested.
nextActions: Keep existing report-work PASS evidence as regression coverage.
Complete material service acceptance for the inventory boundary.
Only add standalone bad-quantity acceptance after product/package evidence exists.
nonSolutions: Do not infer inventory write-back from wom_check_records or QCS state backfill.
Do not force wom_mat_consum_recods rows with a database trigger for the current manual-putin path.

items.title: Material warehouse/inventory service package is missing

persistenceCoverage.operation: 不良数、完工入库/库存等剩余生产写动作落库验收
persistenceCoverage.api: No dedicated bad-quantity endpoint recovered; material-service inventory/stock-in endpoints remain blocked. WOM reject-material prePareReject/materiaReject/batchReject, checkoutBill/generate quality activity, and QCS treatment paths are accepted.

recheckCommands: manual: after material service deployment, run checkProdResult/generateProduceOutSing marker flows and query material stock/inbound tables
passCriteria: serviceName=material or the confirmed warehouse service has a healthy Nacos prod instance.
checkProdResult and generateProduceOutSing no longer return 503.
Controller/service/DAO/SQL path is traced.
PostgreSQL target inventory or stock-in tables show marker writes.
nextActions: Deploy and register the material warehouse/inventory package.
Build the material-service table map.
Run marker stock-in/inventory acceptance before changing PROD-022 to PASS.
nonSolutions: Do not treat BaseSet material master data or WOM internal material tables as the material-service stock-in target.
Do not mark PASS from HTTP 200 alone without database write proof.

items.title: Production list data export implementation is missing
recheckCommands: manual: after export button/backend implementation is recovered, capture browser file response, filename, size and workbook sample
passCriteria: Product owner confirms which production lists must support data export.
Runtime button/menu metadata exposes an export action for each required list.
Target source/runtime sourceAudit finds exportExcel/excelExport/导出 hooks for each required list.
Backend export endpoints return non-empty files with list data, not empty template-download placeholders.
PostgreSQL persistence is NOT_APPLICABLE for file export; acceptance evidence must prove browser file response and sample workbook content.
nextActions: Confirm export scope with product owner.
Recover or implement data-export service methods and runtime buttons for required lists; current public browser ingress already proves page reachability for all 6 target lists.
Run real browser file-response and sourceAudit acceptance before changing PROD-023 to PASS.
nonSolutions: Do not accept empty HTTP 200 downloadXls responses as data export.
Do not confuse import-template download with list-data export.
